title: Make illegal states unrepresentable in types
description: Use ADTs, opaque types, and refined types so invalid data cannot be constructed rather than validated late.
body: Use when modeling a domain with constrained values or finite states. Do not represent a finite set of states as loose strings/ints or carry validity as a separate boolean. Actions: encode states as a sealed ADT/enum, wrap primitives in opaque types or value classes to prevent mixing (a UserId is not any String), and push validation to the constructor (smart constructor returning Either) so a value's existence proves its validity. Validation signals include a test that an invalid construction fails to compile or returns a Left, and that downstream code needs no re-validation. Risk boundary: stringly-typed domains let mismatched values flow through silently; a value class/opaque type that is unwrapped too early reintroduces the confusion it prevented.
